alternatives.name: CNC-machined from alloy bar (in-house or job shop)
alternatives.tradeoffs: ~65% share; 50-70% material waste; high per-unit machining cost; in-house capital and programming burden; no die lead time; full geometry flexibility; operator-dependent consistency; +-0.05-0.1 mm typical job-shop tolerance [SRC: JTBD_generator_keybars]

alternatives.name: Cold-drawn near-net-shape profile (ZOLLERN path)
alternatives.tradeoffs: ~20% share; 6-12 wk die lead time; 100 kg min; 40-70% machining waste eliminated; IT8 (+-0.02 mm) die-controlled tolerance; grain-flow-aligned fatigue endurance; batch consistency [SRC: JTBD_generator_keybars]

alternatives.name: Standard catalog steel profile stock
alternatives.tradeoffs: ~10% share; limited geometry; immediate availability; secondary machining required; unknown metallurgical state; no custom geometry [SRC: VN_generator_keybars]

alternatives.name: Welded / fabricated keybar
alternatives.tradeoffs: ~5% share; HAZ stress concentrations; poor dimensional accuracy; fatigue risk at welds; retrofit/maintenance only; not viable for new OEM build [SRC: JTBD_generator_keybars]

alternatives.name: Do nothing / continue in-house machining (status quo)
alternatives.tradeoffs: Zero switching cost; zero new qualification burden; full geometry control; tradeoff: high material waste, in-house capacity tied up
